prompt: The catch block catches the wrong exception type. What is the bug?
choices: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException and NullPointerException are the same class
The catch block only catches NullPointerException; ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is not caught and propagates up, terminating the program
catch(NullPointerException) also catches all subclasses of RuntimeException
arr[10] never throws because Java auto-resizes arrays
explanation: arr[10] throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException, which is not a NullPointerException. The catch block does not match, so the exception propagates uncaught. Fix: catch(ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException e) or catch(RuntimeException e) to catch both.
